requestedDecision: Adversarially review the GH-51 change that makes rigor consult-decide persist each independent-review decision as an append-only review-decisions artifact. Look for correctness bugs, a broken selector-purity boundary, schema violations, exit-code regressions, or any way persistence could become an authorization to transmit or an approval. Read-only.
requiredActions: README still states consult-decide is pure, but the command now performs filesystem I/O by default; clarify that only the decision selection is pure while the command persists by default.
Persistence test does not validate the saved artifact against independent-review-decision.v1 (additionalProperties/required) nor assert a UUID-suffixed filename; a schema regression would pass.
Dry-run test does not assert stdout preserves the pure decision output or exit codes; existing exit-code test already covers stop=2/request=0.
